message: Node started: Perform a systematic search to find the least prime p such that there exists an integer n satisfying n^4 + 1 = 0 (mod p^2). To do this, test primes p in strictly increasing order starting from p = 2. For each prime p, first check if there exists an n such that n^4 + 1 = 0 (mod p). If such an n exists, then check if there exists an n such that n^4 + 1 = 0 (mod p^2). You must explicitly state that you have checked all primes smaller than your candidate p to guarantee it is the least. Once the least prime p is found, output only the integer value of p.
